key_insight: Shape recovery and droplet fusion in biomolecular condensates are both capillary-driven, but viscoelastic stress relaxation makes them fundamentally different relaxation processes.
engineering_relevance: Models for condensate dynamics must include stress relaxation and surrounding-fluid dissipation to predict fusion and recovery times accurately in soft-matter and biomedical systems.
method: Analytical small-deformation theory for single-droplet shape recovery
Axisymmetric finite-element simulations using the Oldroyd-B constitutive model
Systematic comparison of Newtonian vs. viscoelastic droplets under matched conditions
evidence_signal: The paper combines theory and simulation and reports that viscoelastic droplets show multistage fusion, enhanced intermediate-stage dynamics, and deviations from stretched-exponential fits relative to Newtonian cases.
watch_item: The Oldroyd-B model and stretched-exponential fits may miss additional material nonlinearities or interfacial effects in real biomolecular condensates.
paper_title: Droplet Fusion as a Relaxation Process: Comparison with Shape Recovery of Newtonian and Viscoelastic Droplets
